name: Kandji MDM API
what_it_does: Apple-focused MDM platform with REST API for managing macOS, iOS, iPadOS, and tvOS devices. Provides automated device enrollment, configuration, security policy enforcement, and compliance monitoring.
use_cases: Automating device enrollment and configuration profiles via API for IT onboarding agents
Querying device compliance status and remediation actions programmatically
Managing software library deployments and patches across Apple fleet
Syncing user-device assignments with HR/identity systems
Triggering remote lock, wipe, or restart actions on managed devices
not_for: Managing Windows or Android devices (Kandji is Apple-only)
Organizations without Apple devices — use Intune or Workspace ONE
best_when: An agent manages Apple device fleets and needs to automate compliance checks, software deployment, and device lifecycle operations.
avoid_when: You manage mixed Windows/Apple fleets — use Microsoft Intune or Jamf Pro instead.

auth.notes: API tokens from Kandji admin portal with configurable read/write scopes per resource type. Tokens are long-lived and scoped to specific API endpoints. Subdomain-based API URLs per tenant.

pricing.notes: Per-device monthly pricing. Enterprise plan includes all features. Minimum device count requirements may apply. Trial available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI specification published. REST API well-documented. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Webhooks for device enrollment and compliance events enable reactive agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device-level operations are generally idempotent. Profile assignments and library item deployments are idempotent by design.
known_agent_gotchas: Tenant subdomain required in all API URLs — agents must store per-tenant endpoint
Device commands are async — status must be polled after issuing remote actions
Blueprint and library item IDs must be known before referencing — catalog them first
Apple APNs certificates must be valid — expired certificates break all MDM communication

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. TLS enforced. Scoped API tokens. Apple MDM protocol uses certificate-based device identity. Data processed in US-based infrastructure.
